thread.title: How much are Highway 407 ETR prices going up in 2025?
thread.title_full: How much are Highway 407 ETR prices going up in 2025?

author: user
title: How much are Highway 407 ETR prices going up in 2025?
text: Highway 407 ETR is increasing tolls and fees as well as introducing more toll zones, vehicle classifications and time-of-use rates. The changes come into effect on Jan. 1, 2025. Drivers of light vehicles will notice tolls increase between three and 14 cents per hour, depending on the time of day and zone traveled in, the highway operator announced Nov. 22. Various fees are also going up, with the highway operator saying most personal transponder customers can expect an increase of $8 to their monthly bill. “With the gridlock issues facing the GTA today, our service has never been more important. We’re working hard to keep traffic free flowing, and attracting more drivers in sections of the highway that can afford a bit more traffic — without disrupting the world-class driving experience that motorists rely on,” says Javier Tamargo, president and CEO of 407 ETR, in a news release. Zones As of Jan. 1, the highway will have 12 zones, up from the current four, with different toll rates for each. This is intended to better manage traffic volumes and flow, the highway operator said. New vehicle classifications There will also be new vehicle classifications added, with rates intended to better reflect the vehicle’s impact on the road. The new classifications include motorcycles and medium-sized vehicles. Vehicles with two or three wheels fall into the motorcycle category while larger pickup trucks, Sprinter-style vans and SUVs, trucks and vans towing a truck fall into the medium category. Rates are as follows: - Motorcycles – 0.8x of light vehicle rate - Medium – 1.5x of light vehicle rate - Heavy Single – 2x of light vehicle rate - Heavy Multiple – 3x of light vehicle rate Most personal vehicles, such as cars, vans, SUVs and small pickup truck fall into the light vehicle category. Heavy Single includes single-unit vehicles, tractors and buses, while Heavy Multiple consists of trucks and tractors with one or more trailers. New rates Highway 407 published its new rates for each zone for each time period, with toll rates ranging from the mid-30s to the mid-80s in cents per kilometre traveled. The rate chart is available on 407etr.com . Drivers with a transponder don’t have to pay camera charges and account fees, with the highway operator saying the transponder pays for itself after three round trips. Starting Jan. 1, an annual transponder lease will cost $29.50 plus tax and additional transponders can be leased on the same account at a price of $11.95 plus tax each. Highway 407 spans 108 kilometres between Burlington and Pickering and is owned by 407 International Inc. The highway operator has a toll calculator available on its website . Error!
